$schema-note: Corpus manifest for the Plugin Verifier compatibility regression harness. Sources: 'builtin' (text-only tree under fixtures/<id>/content, packaged deterministically at run time; directories named *.jar/*.zip become nested archives), 'url' (public artifact downloaded and verified against sha256; only used with --remote), 'local' (artifact supplied on the local filesystem, never committed).

fixtures.description: Minimal valid plugin distributed as a single JAR with META-INF/plugin.xml.
Classic ZIP distribution: <plugin>/lib/<plugin>.jar containing the descriptor.
ZIP whose lib/ holds the plugin JAR plus a descriptor-less third-party library JAR; exercises descriptor discovery among multiple JARs.
Two JARs in lib/, each with its own META-INF/plugin.xml declaring different plugin ids; ambiguous descriptor.
plugin.xml with unclosed tags (not well-formed XML).
Descriptor assembled via xi:include from a sibling file in META-INF, contributing extensions and actions.
Optional <depends> with config-file present in META-INF; exercises optional descriptor resolution.
Optional <depends> pointing to a config-file that does not exist.
V2 plugin model: package attribute, <content> module with its own descriptor at the classpath root, <dependencies> on plugin and module.
Suspicious compatibility range: ancient since-build 139.0 and magic until-build 999.*.
Theme plugin: themeProvider extension pointing at a JSON theme at the JAR root.
ZIP where the plugin JAR sits directly under the top-level directory instead of lib/.
plugin.xml with a UTF-8 byte-order mark and non-ASCII plugin name.
Plugin supplied as an exploded directory with lib/<plugin>.jar, not as an archive.
V2 descriptor with two separate <dependencies> blocks (MP-8208, PR #1525); older parsers ignore all but one block.
